reviews.review_title: A Better, more robust Angry Birds
reviews.review_description:
Love all the ammunition options and unlockable content, the addition of the wand to fend off attacks adds a layer of complexity which I dig. Only downfall I've felt where Angry Birds has this beat is the actual physics of the towers/walls when hit. I'd love to see more real world physics in toppling or setting up chain reactions.
But overall a solid game with a lot of replay factor if you dig this sort of game

Don’t buy this game it’s a total waste of money it sucks and it’s boring because when I played it was buried all you do is put an animal in a slingshot then you just fire it at something and I destroyed you look at one to five

reviews.review_title: Did not explain how to get more ammo
reviews.review_description: This game is not it. I’ve been on the same stage for months/ it dis not explain good how to get additional ammunition and what am I going to be able to do with three minions??? I’m over it!
Very good price for the quality and attention to detail that has been implemented in this game. It is sort of like angry birds but better.

I can't get past the unicorn horn. Seriously I've had this game for months and can't play it.
Total waist of money.
I don't understand how to move forward. I'm mad I spent money on this. Can't do a thing.

I agree with most of the 3-5 star reviews. However, one thing that no one seems to mention that ruined the experience for me is the fact that after the first map, every level thereafter is a race against time. Buildings can regenerate and I've found myself frantically looking for the right ammo and the correct target(s). You won't be able to take your time after what is essentially the tutorial. The amount of buildings that self repair can be numerous. They don't just repair either; they get reinforced. Just FYI.

Ballista is a "Crush the Castle" / "Angry Birds" type game, very well made and with enough twists in the game play and style to make it unique.

Levels are well thought out, with a nice variety of difficulty. There are challenge levels, and multiple goals to achieve which gives more than enough content for your money.

Graphics are cute with sharp textures and resolution. Sounds are fun and the voice acting is pretty good too. Minor frame rate issues (like all games I've played)

When compared to Angry Birds VR, I would say Ballista is the better game.

This is a cute game, a clean happy game for kids of all ages and I’m sure many adults would enjoy it also. It’s basically Angry birds leveled down. Graphics are good but not amazing, very cartoony. Angry birds graphics are much better and the comedy of the birds has a bigger smile factor. However the game is reasonably priced and great to let kids play.
Y

This is a great game. I’m about one third a way through the game and I love it but there was one thing that happens shortly after I unlocked the sea of grass. I think that’s the name of it. After unlocking the sea of grass I played through the first level and decided to take a break. But the next I come on I come on to see the mirror playing the slideshow at the beginning of the game. So to say the least I was confused. Then I open up the map and there is NO PROGRESS! I had to start all the way form the beginning. I’m not taking away a star because I think that this doesn’t merit lose if a star but whoever made this game, please tell me what went wrong I just want to know. This is a great game that I definitely recommend playing.
Fun but I wish there were more levels

reviews.review_title: Why is all the UI so FAR away?!
reviews.review_description:
I am knocking a star off for the UI being SOOOO far from your body. This game is basically impossible to play sitting (And therefor not accessible by many people, including wheelchairs and those with limited mobility) because it puts all the stuff on your left and right super far away. I have about a 6ft wingspan, and I have to reach as far as I can to the side to grab it. I tried to play this in a limited space one time since I figured a simple slingshot game wouldn't take a large area, but I was wrong. I physically couldn't extend my arm far enough to grab ammo or I'd hit a wall in my house. I had to hold the realign button to spin the camera and put the ammo in front of me, then realign the quest again a 2nd time to replace the slingshot in front of me so I can grab it. 

Why did they put everything to far apart, with almost no settings in the game at all?! It just seems bizarre.  Game is pretty decent otherwise, I'd rate 4 if it was more accessible and contained more customizable settings. Or just put in the Jedi Force/Harry Potter Accio system lots of other games have, where you just reach and grab for something and you summon it to your hand!
It is an overall nice game, a bit like angry birds in 3D, but the pedestals are really low and quite far away, so I, and probably many others would appreciate som scale settings
better angry birds with new fun aspects
Similar to a angry birds
There's quite a lot going on here. Lots of unique ammo types and special charms. Cute, colourful graphics. Good value. Hours of entertainment with Campaign and Challenges.

reviews.review_title: Excellent game that needs admin polish
reviews.review_description: The gameplay is excellent and the graphics are fun and the sense of scale is great standing behind the ballista.   However the game needs profile support. My wife and I both want to play but it’s anticlimactic for one of us to simply “replay” a level when the treasure chests have already been opened.
The game is fun but like another reviewer mentioned it can get annoying waiting for through the transitions.  Would be nice to be able to skip them or speed them up.
I absolutely love this game so far, its so creative and well put together, super easy to play and funny dialouge and awesome spells and ammo I would 100% recommend this to my friends

I love this game. From the graphics (popping colors) and character design to the gameplay...it's awesome. Like Angry Birds you need to fire characters at a structure to bring it down and kill the enemy. Angry Birds uses a slingshot. Ballista uses a catapult. Angry Birds has just a few characters to shoot through the air. Ballista, well... So far I've only played a few levels and I already have 7 or 8 different characters and some spells to choose from. On top of that you have a magic wand which appears in your hand when you stick your hand into the air and pull the trigger. A magic wand? Yes, or better yet: The horn of a unicorn to shoot down enemy projectiles. Again: I love this game.
